title: 6 most snake-filled water bodies in South Carolina: From the Savannah River to the ACE Basin
summary_full: South Carolina's abundant waters attract many snake species, both harmless and dangerous. The Savannah River and Great Pee Dee River are home to several notable snake populations. Congaree River and Lake Murray host a variety of snakes, including venomous ones. Lake Hartwell and the ACE Basin also feature numerous water-dwelling snake species. These warm environments and plentiful prey make South Carolina ideal for snakes.
